Artificial Intelligence Applications (Workflow
Optimization, Research Assistance)

Familiar with
SQL
Python (for Data Analysis)
Soft Skills
Strong Communication: Strengthened through direct client interactions as a Property Advisor and during the
development of Bio Klick, where clear communication was key to understanding client needs and presenting
customized digital solutions.
Adaptable & Fast Learner: Proven ability to quickly master new tools and technologies across diverse roles — from
financial systems in accounting to AI concepts and data analytics in IBM and HP LIFE certifications.
Analytical Thinking: Strengthened through analyzing market data as a Property Advisor and financial reports as
an Assistant Accountant, leading to informed decisions and improved accuracy.
Creativity & Innovation: Shown by designing unique, responsive websites and developing NFC-integrated solutions
through Bio Klick, merging technology with personalized branding.
Problem-Solving: Built through identifying system and client challenges in real estate and web development
projects, then proposing tailored, effective solutions.
Teamwork: Gained through collaborating with marketing and operations teams at The Address and leading small
teams for web projects at Bio Klick to deliver cohesive results.
Attention to Detail: Demonstrated through ensuring accuracy in financial reports, maintaining organized client
data, and delivering precise, responsive web designs tailored to user needs.
Time Management: Refined by balancing multiple projects, academic work, and part-time roles simultaneously
while meeting deadlines and maintaining performance.
Self-Motivation: Demonstrated by founding Bio Klick, managing all aspects from branding to client relations, and
continuously pursuing professional certifications to expand technical knowledge.
Project Management: Strengthened through practical training and real project involvement, including planning
support, task coordination, testing follow-up, and production deployment assistance.

Jana Bassem Amin
Phone number: 01032500554 Email: Jana.amin@student.guc.edu.eg
Linkedin : www.linkedin.com/in/jana-bassem-553a43271
Career Objective:
Motivated and detail oriented Business Informatics student with a strong passion for Data Science,
Data Engineering and AI. Skilled in Python, Java, MySQL, SAP, and Data Analysis with hands on
experience across multiple real world projects. Actively seeking an internship opportunity to apply
my technical, analytical, and business knowledge in a professional setting.

Education:
• Bachelor of Science in Business Informatics
German University in Cairo | 2022- present
• IGCSE Certificate
Egyptian British International School |2022 | 100%

Work Experience
• Data Science and Artificial Intelligence Intern at E& Egypt (Aug 2025-Sep 2025)
Built end to end Data Analysis and modelling pipelines in Python including EDA on datasets and
machine learning tasks. Performed Data Engineering with PySpark on Databricks.
• Data Engineer intern at Credit Agricole Egypt (Aug 2024-Sep 2024)
Assisted with database optimization and supported the data management team in implementing ETL
workflows, Data Analysis using SQL and created OBIEE dashboards.
Technical Skills :
• Programming: Java, Python, SQL, ABAP and C#
• Tools & Platforms: Eclipse, VS Code, MySQL, Jupyter , Oracle, DataBricks , SAP , Salesforce, Tableau
• Concepts: OOP, Data Engineering , RESTful APIs, Data Analysis and Visualization using Python ,
Agile, Digital Transformation , EDA Analysis , Machine Learning , ERP Systems .
• Web Development: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, ASP.NET
• Soft Skills: Problem Solving, Leadership, Time Management, Communication

Projects:
• Attack on Titans Game : (OOP, Java , Data structures) (Dec 2023 – Mar 2024): Built a character
based game from scratch
• Telecom Database System (Sep 2024 – Dec 2024): Designed a relational database using SQL.
• Admin-Customer Web Portal (Sep 2024 – Dec 2024): Created an ASP.NET website
• SAP ABAP Project (Feb 2025 – May 2025): Built interactive SAP applications including dialogs, data
dictionaries, and GUI elements.
• Internship Management Web App (Feb 2025 – May 2025): Developed a web application using
HTML, CSS, and JS for internship tracking.
• IT Project Plan (Feb 2025 – May 2025): Created and managed a project timeline using MS Project.
• ML model (Oct 2025 –Nov 2025): Built a Linear Regression model to predict Energy Consumption

Certifications:
• Artificial Intelligence & Machine learning with Python - SPRINTS x Microsoft Summer Camp (2025)
• Data Science & Analytics – HP Life (2025)

Extracurricular Activities:
• Member at the university student Committee (2022-Present)
• Member of the Media Team at IEEE GUC and Athar GUC (Sep 2024-Sep 2025)
• Participated as an usher in the GUC Graduation 2024 and GUC Orientation 2025

full_text:
Karen Fady Nabil
Aspiring Cybersecurity Professional | Penetration Testing & Network Security
01126450165 • karenfadywork@gmail.com • Cairo, Egypt • linkedin.com/in/karen-fady • github.com/Toxkay • tryhackme.com/p/ToxKay
PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Third-year IT student at Cairo University specializing in penetration testing and network security, with 180+ hours of hands-on
training in web application hacking and enterprise network hardening. Experienced in exploiting OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities
using Burp Suite and OWASP ZAP, and in designing secure network infrastructures using Cisco tooling. Ranked in the top 25%
on TryHackMe, actively sharpening offensive security skills through real-world lab environments.
EDUCATION
BSc in Computers and Artificial Intelligence — Information Technology
Expected 2027
Cairo University, Cairo, Egypt
CERTIFICATIONS & TRAINING
eWPT Preparation Course | eLearnSecurity
60+ Hours
• Applied OWASP-based methodology for comprehensive web application security assessments
• Manually exploited SQLi, XSS, LFI/RFI, and Command Injection vulnerabilities in realistic lab environments
• Performed OSINT and passive reconnaissance to uncover hidden directories, tech stacks, and weak components
• Tested for broken authentication, misconfigurations, and simulated real-world attack scenarios
Network Security | National Telecommunication Institute (NTI), Egypt
Jul 2025 – Aug 2025
120+ hours of technical training
• Covered CompTIA Security+, CCNA, and hands-on labs with Nmap, Wireshark, and Cisco Packet Tracer
• Configured routers, switches, VLANs, ACLs, and built secure network topologies using Cisco tools
• Gained practical experience in VPNs, firewalls, RADIUS/TACACS+, routing protocols (OSPF, RIP), and device hardening
• Designed and implemented a solo final project simulating a multi-area secure enterprise network environment
DevOps Foundations Crash Course | Orange Digital Center Egypt
1 month
• Hands-on exposure to Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, and Prometheus & Grafana
• Introduced to CI/CD pipeline automation using Jenkins with foundational knowledge of modern software deployment
WORK EXPERIENCE
IT Help-Desk Technician | Patriarchal College, Cairo, Egypt
Sep 2024 – Dec 2024
• Resolved hardware and software issues across student and staff devices running Windows and Linux
• Installed, configured, and maintained desktop systems, printers, and peripherals
• Provided first-level server support including backups, service restarts, and user account management
• Developed practical understanding of DHCP, DNS, and file sharing protocols in a live environment
PROJECTS
Network Security Infrastructure Simulation (NETSEC) — Solo Project
• Designed and configured a multi-zone enterprise network using Cisco Packet Tracer, demonstrating real-world network
architecture understanding
• Implemented Zone-Based Policy Firewalls (ZPF) and VPNs for secure remote connectivity
• Configured AAA (RADIUS/TACACS+) and ACLs to enforce role-based access control
Vole Machine – CPU Architecture Simulator — Academic Project
• Developed a C++ simulation of a CPU with a custom instruction set, 256-cell memory, and registers R0–RC
• Simulated the full Fetch-Decode-Execute cycle, supporting arithmetic, logic, and program control operations
• Implemented custom 8-bit floating-point and two's complement integer formats
Plateful – Collaborative Web Application — Academic Project
• Built a full-stack recipe management system using Django (Python) and JavaScript
• Implemented secure user authentication with custom sign-up flows and encrypted session handling
• Managed data persistence using Django ORM and SQLite

TECHNICAL SKILLS
Vulnerability Assessment: OWASP Top 10, SQLi, XSS (Stored/Reflected/DOM), CSRF, SSRF, LFI/RFI, Command Injection
Offensive Security Tools: Burp Suite Professional, OWASP ZAP, ffuf, Nmap, Wireshark
Network Security: OSPF, RIP, VLANs, IP Subnetting, ACLs, Zone-Based Policy Firewalls (ZPF), VPN, AAA
(RADIUS/TACACS+)
Protocols: TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, NAT, HTTP/S
Programming Languages: Python, C++, JavaScript, PHP, SQL, Java
CS Fundamentals: Data Structures & Algorithms, Object-Oriented Programming (OOP), SOLID Principles
DevOps & Environments: Git, Linux (Kali / Ubuntu), VMware / VirtualBox, Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, Jenkins
LANGUAGES
Arabic (Native) • English (Academic / Professional) • French (DELF B2)

Karen Fady Nabil
Aspiring Cybersecurity Professional | Penetration Testing & Network Security
01126450165 • karenfadywork@gmail.com • Cairo, Egypt • linkedin.com/in/karen-fady • github.com/Toxkay • tryhackme.com/p/ToxKay
PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Third-year IT student at Cairo University specializing in penetration testing and network security, with 180+ hours of hands-on
training in web application hacking and enterprise network hardening. Experienced in exploiting OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities
using Burp Suite and OWASP ZAP, and in designing secure network infrastructures using Cisco tooling. Ranked in the top 25%
on TryHackMe, actively sharpening offensive security skills through real-world lab environments.

sections.heading: Education
sections.content:
BSc in Computers and Artificial Intelligence — Information Technology
Expected 2027
Cairo University, Cairo, Egypt
CERTIFICATIONS & TRAINING
eWPT Preparation Course | eLearnSecurity
60+ Hours
• Applied OWASP-based methodology for comprehensive web application security assessments
• Manually exploited SQLi, XSS, LFI/RFI, and Command Injection vulnerabilities in realistic lab environments
• Performed OSINT and passive reconnaissance to uncover hidden directories, tech stacks, and weak components
• Tested for broken authentication, misconfigurations, and simulated real-world attack scenarios
Network Security | National Telecommunication Institute (NTI), Egypt
Jul 2025 – Aug 2025
120+ hours of technical training
• Covered CompTIA Security+, CCNA, and hands-on labs with Nmap, Wireshark, and Cisco Packet Tracer
• Configured routers, switches, VLANs, ACLs, and built secure network topologies using Cisco tools
• Gained practical experience in VPNs, firewalls, RADIUS/TACACS+, routing protocols (OSPF, RIP), and device hardening
• Designed and implemented a solo final project simulating a multi-area secure enterprise network environment
DevOps Foundations Crash Course | Orange Digital Center Egypt
1 month
• Hands-on exposure to Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, and Prometheus & Grafana
• Introduced to CI/CD pipeline automation using Jenkins with foundational knowledge of modern software deployment

sections.heading: Work Experience
sections.content:
IT Help-Desk Technician | Patriarchal College, Cairo, Egypt
Sep 2024 – Dec 2024
• Resolved hardware and software issues across student and staff devices running Windows and Linux
• Installed, configured, and maintained desktop systems, printers, and peripherals
• Provided first-level server support including backups, service restarts, and user account management
• Developed practical understanding of DHCP, DNS, and file sharing protocols in a live environment

sections.heading: Projects
sections.content:
Network Security Infrastructure Simulation (NETSEC) — Solo Project
• Designed and configured a multi-zone enterprise network using Cisco Packet Tracer, demonstrating real-world network
architecture understanding
• Implemented Zone-Based Policy Firewalls (ZPF) and VPNs for secure remote connectivity
• Configured AAA (RADIUS/TACACS+) and ACLs to enforce role-based access control
Vole Machine – CPU Architecture Simulator — Academic Project
• Developed a C++ simulation of a CPU with a custom instruction set, 256-cell memory, and registers R0–RC
• Simulated the full Fetch-Decode-Execute cycle, supporting arithmetic, logic, and program control operations
• Implemented custom 8-bit floating-point and two's complement integer formats
Plateful – Collaborative Web Application — Academic Project
• Built a full-stack recipe management system using Django (Python) and JavaScript
• Implemented secure user authentication with custom sign-up flows and encrypted session handling
• Managed data persistence using Django ORM and SQLite

sections.heading: Technical Skills
sections.content:
Vulnerability Assessment: OWASP Top 10, SQLi, XSS (Stored/Reflected/DOM), CSRF, SSRF, LFI/RFI, Command Injection
Offensive Security Tools: Burp Suite Professional, OWASP ZAP, ffuf, Nmap, Wireshark
Network Security: OSPF, RIP, VLANs, IP Subnetting, ACLs, Zone-Based Policy Firewalls (ZPF), VPN, AAA
(RADIUS/TACACS+)
Protocols: TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, NAT, HTTP/S
Programming Languages: Python, C++, JavaScript, PHP, SQL, Java
CS Fundamentals: Data Structures & Algorithms, Object-Oriented Programming (OOP), SOLID Principles
DevOps & Environments: Git, Linux (Kali / Ubuntu), VMware / VirtualBox, Docker, Kubernetes, Ansible, Jenkins

sections.heading: Languages
sections.content: Arabic (Native) • English (Academic / Professional) • French (DELF B2)

PROFILE
A results-oriented Business Informatics student with a deep interest in how all departments connect to create a successful business. I

2022 – 2026
Egypt British International School, IGCSE
O-Levels & AS Mathematics
2012 – 2022
PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

I manage all financial operations, from daily accounting and product pricing to strategic budgeting for
best and worst-case scenarios. I built the company’s financial foundation from scratch to ensure steady
growth and smart spending
09/2025 – Present
Digital Transformation Intern, Triforce
Gained hands-on experience in Digital Transformation, focusing on automating business processes
through Robotic Process Automation (RPA).
05/2023 – 08/2023
EXTRA CURRICULAR ACTIVITIES
Customer Experience Team Leader, AIESEC
• Managed travel logistics for incoming volunteers (visas, flights, schedules).

• Established key partnerships and monitored volunteer engagement.

• Coordinated with Marketing, Business Development, Logistics, and Delegate Experience teams.
• Conducted an analysis of achieved vs. planned finances.
Member Special Event Organizing Committee, AIESEC National Conference
• Developed partnerships with performers and companies.
• Organized event agenda and logistics.
PR Manager Regional Presidents Meeting’24, AIESEC Regional Conference
• Established media agency partnerships.
• Developed collaborations with Ministries & Governmental stakeholders.
R&D Team Member & Speaker, Hewidy Groups

the R&D section.

Soft skills
Communication Skills · Team Player· Problem Solving · Leadership · Multitasking · Time Management· Crisis Management

Microsoft Office · Google Docs · Visual Studio · FL Studio · RPA (UI Path) · Canva

Business Fundamentals Marketing Strategy, Udemy

Courses and Training

• Machine Learning Diploma - CSKILLED ACADEMY: 130+ hour
Projects
§ NYC Taxi Trip Duration Prediction
• Developed Ridge Regression model with polynomial features achieving R² = 0.74, RMSE = 316s on 1M records
• Engineered spatial (Haversine, Manhattan distance) and temporal features (time buckets, weekend flags) with
robust outlier handling
§ Credit Card Fraud Detection
• Built XGBoost classifier achieving F1-score of 0.88 with Precision 0.89 and Recall 0.88 on imbalanced dataset
• Engineered temporal features (hour sin/cos encoding, weekend flags) and handled class imbalance using
scale pos weight
• Evaluated model performance using ROC-AUC, PR-AUC curves, and comprehensive metrics across train/val/test
splits
§ Multi-Class Classification with Neural Network
• Implemented Multi-Layer Perceptron with 3 hidden layers (40-20-10 neurons) using Scikit-learn’s MLPClassifier
• Applied data normalization, 80/20 train-test split, and visualized model performance using learning curves.
§ Linear Regression & Gradient Descent from Scratch
• Implemented Linear Regression using 3 approaches: analytical Normal Equations, custom Gradient Descent, and
Scikit-learn comparison
• Built gradient descent optimizer from scratch with configurable learning rate and convergence tracking,
visualizing cost function convergence
Relevant Tools & Skills
• Programming: Python (PyTorch, Scikit-learn, NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
• Deep Learning & Computer Vision: CNNs, RNNs, LSTMs, Neural Networks, Image Classification.

SUMMARY
Motivated Computer Science student still learning to become a Backend .NET Core Developer, with strong coding, problem-solving, and
AI skills. Proficient in C# with hands-on experience in software development and data structures. Passionate about building scalable,
efficient solutions and continuously learning new technologies. Seeking opportunities to contribute to impactful backend projects.
EXPERIENCE
7/24 – 8/24
Vodafone Summer Internship
contributed to the planning and design of cloud infrastructure, enhancing network capabilities and
operational efficiency. (Tools: VMware ESXi, vSphere).
EDUCATION
9/22 – 7/26
Ain Shams University
Bachelor of Computer and Information Science (CGPA: 3.486)
PROJECTS
8/25 – 9/25
Course Management System
ASP.NET Core MVC and Web API
• Developed a full-stack application to manage courses, students, instructors, and enrollments using EF Core
with one-to-many and many-to-many relationships.
• Implemented MVC features: views, controllers, ViewData/​ViewBag/​ViewModels, form handling, client-side
and custom validations, partial views, and Ajax.
• Built a Web API layer with GET/​POST/​PUT/​DELETE endpoints, using repository pattern and dependency
injection to share business logic between MVC and API.
Technologies: ASP.NET Core MVC, EF Core, C#, Web API, SQL Server.
3/25 – 5/25
Steam Video Game Sales Prediction
• Merged and cleaned multiple Steam datasets to analyze game features, genres, platform support,
achievements, and user reviews.
• Built and trained machine learning models to predict game sales and identify key factors driving success.
• Visualized insights to uncover patterns in user engagement and commercial performance.
Technologies: Python, pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, Matplotlib, Seaborn, Jupyter Notebook.
10/24 – 01/25
FCIS ASU Operating System
Developed multi-threaded programs, simulated CPU scheduling algorithms (FCFS, Round Robin, Priority),
implemented thread synchronization using mutexes and semaphores, and built basic virtual memory/​page
replacement simulations.
Technologies: C
3/24 – 5/24
Fraud Detection Model
Built a machine learning system to identify and prevent fraudulent activities in transactions. Performed data
cleaning, feature engineering, and evaluated performance with metrics like precision, recall, and F1-score.
Technologies: Python, pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, Jupyter Notebook.
4/23 – 7/23
Online shopping System
Developed a desktop application enabling buyers to browse, purchase, and rate products, and sellers to
manage inventory. Implemented structured programming concepts with a database for persistent data storage.
Features include order tracking, product search with autocomplete, subscriptions to sellers, and coupon-based
discounts.
Technologies: C++, Qt, MySQL, Structured Programming.

7/25

Essam AbdelNabi (Metigator)

programming, and LINQ for querying collections.

MVC & Web APIs: Developing structured web applications and robust backend services.
7/24 – 8/24
Computer Network Fundamentals
Mahara-Tech
Learned key networking concepts including network components, topologies, LAN/​WAN, OSI and TCP/​IP
models, and basic network configuration and troubleshooting.
2/23 – 7/23
C++ Programming Basics
Mohamed Abo-Hadhoud (Programming Advices)

vectors, and functions.
• Gained hands-on experience with Object-Oriented Programming: classes, objects, constructors/​destructors,
inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation.
• Learned advanced concepts such as pointers, references, memory management, and debugging techniques.

full_text:
Mai Waseem
New Cairo, Egypt | 010122906434 | maiwassem01@gmail.com

Business Informatics student at the German University in Cairo with strong analytical and
operational skills. Experienced in performance monitoring, data analysis, incentive and process
optimization using Excel, SQL, and data visualization tools. Seeking an Operations or Fintech-
focused internship, and data-driven decision making to support growth and facilitate data-driven
decision making.
Education:
Bachelor Degree in Business Informatics
German University in Cairo (GUC)
Expected Graduation: 2027 | GPA: 1.79 (in German scale – 1.0 is Excellent)
Key Skills:
Technical: Microsoft Excel (Advanced Modeling, Pivot Tables), SQL (Joins, Subqueries),
Tableau (Data Visualization), Python (Data Wrangling/EDA).
Finance & Ops: Financial Ratio Analysis, Risk & Return Modeling (CAPM), Business Process
Optimization, Strategic Planning.
Soft Skills: Data Storytelling, Cross-functional Collaboration, Problem Solving.

Projects:
 E-Commerce Revenue & Operations Analysis | Winter 2026
- Engineered an end-to-end data pipeline to analyze 1M+ rows of retail transaction data,
identifying a 20% YoY revenue growth totaling $8.68M.
- Developed automated dashboards in Tableau to track KPIs, diagnosing a 23% month-over-
month performance dip and pinpointing specific underperforming product categories.
- Translated complex SQL queries into executive-level visual reports to support operational
strategy and regional sales monitoring.

 Financial Risk & Portfolio Optimization (Fintech) | Winter 2025
- Modeled risk and return profiles for 15 firms across 4 sectors by analyzing 2 years of daily price
data and calculating Standard Deviation and Coefficients of Variation.
- Applied the Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM) to evaluate required returns, utilizing a 5.3%
risk-free rate and 8.5% market premium to justify investment strategies.

Al-Kon (Global) International School in Jeddah
SKILLS
Time Management, Communication skills, Presentation, Multi-tasking, Fast
Learning Skills and Flexible.
Skilled
Java, Python, Prolog, Haskell, C++, VHDL, HTML, SQL Programming Language,
Cisco Packet Tracer, Java script, C and MERN.
Skilled
PROJECTS
Attack On Titans Game Project
Game Project done using Java, Java FX and advanced GUI - implements the study of
multiple courses including Data Structures and Algorithms.
Mario Based Game
Game Project implemented using Unity Game Engine and coded using Visual Studio.
Teacher Assistant Schedule
Designed a Haskell based program that runs the avaliable Teacher assistants
schedules to assign each TA with a Quiz slot.
Pet Companion Car
An Autonomous 4DW Car coded by VHDL that on dectecting an obstacle or pet on
either of it sides - it moves away.
Travelling and Telecome Website
Designed a Travelling Website using HTML and MongoDB and a Telecome Company
Website using MicrosoftSQL and C+
GUC Internship Website
Designed an internship website that is used by SCAD, Student, Faculty supervisor
and Companies in order to manage the internship cycle for all easily and effectively
using C, React and developed Functional and Non functional requirements.
Scheduler and Interpreter System
Designed a program that includes the backend and frontend of an interpreter that
reads txt files and executes their code, First Come First Serve (FCFS), Round Robin
(RR) and Multi Level Feedback System (MLFQ) Schedulers, a memory that saves
processes in it, mutexes that ensure mutual exclusion over the critical resources and
a scheduler that schedules the processes that are in the system.
Fillet-O-Neumann with moves on the side- Processor Design and Architecture
Simulated a fictional processor design and Von Neumann architecture using C.
Media Project (Movie Poster and Voice effects)
Photoshopped my colleague and I onto a movie poster smoothly and alterned audio
clips of my voice into various pitches and echos.
Eventity
Event planning website for GUC.
Embedded Systems Maze
Embedded Systems car moving using Ul Interface, C code and raspberry pico that is
able to navigate a maze.
Tomasulo Dynamic Programming System
implemented a tomasulo system on C code
Temple Rush
Graphics 3D Game done using C++.
PROFILE
4th year Computer Science and Engineering student
at the German University in Cairo with a strong focus
on Machine Learning and data-driven systems.
Equipped with solid foundations in algorithms,
operating systems, computer architecture, and
database systems, and experienced in building full-

about developing scalable machine learning models

Cairo, Egypt | ASU | Sep 2023 – June 2024

transportation in Cairo, offering real-time route calculations, accurate
arrival times, and enhanced journey planning.

Shortest Route Calculation: Provided efficient bus and walking
routes to improve commute times.
User-Friendly Onboarding: Implemented smooth sign-up/login,
personalized user preferences, and trip history.

and Google Maps API.
Scalable Design: Planned future features for microbus and
monorail routes, with potential city expansions.
Technologies: Dart, Flutter, Firebase, Google Maps API, State
Management, Clean Code principles.

full_text:
Mariz Youssef Abdelmessih
Cairo, Egypt | +20 1281984119
mariz.youssef7@gmail.com | LinkedIn | GitHub
Motivated Computer and Systems Engineering graduate with a strong foundation in software development,
embedded systems, and DevOps. Skilled in designing and implementing software applications, with practical
experience gained through academic and training-based projects. Eager to contribute to innovative software
solutions, grow within a collaborative environment, and continuously expand my technical skills.
Education
Bachelor of Engineering in Computer and Systems Engineering - Ain Shams University
•
GPA: 3.02 / 4.00 – Very Good 2020 – 2025
High School, Good Shepherd Sisters English School 2006 – 2020
•
Grade: 98.7%
Projects
Graduation Project
An Eclipse-based AUTOSAR Tool (Grade: A)
Sponsored by Garraio, LLC — Supervised by Dr. Sherif Hammad
•
Developed a Java-based AUTOSAR authoring tool using ARTOP (AUTOSAR Tool Platform) as a
foundation for model handling and validation.
•
Applied Eclipse Rich Client Platform (RCP) to create a GUI for configuring Atomic SWCs, Interfaces,
Ports, and nested Composition Structures.
•
Enabled the automated generation of ARXML files, as well as RTE-compliant code: RTE.c, RTE.h,
SWCs.c, and SWC.h, following AUTOSAR RTE_SWS specifications.
Other Projects:
JobHuntly – Job Hunting & Recruitment Platform
•
Developed a full-stack recruitment platform connecting job seekers and companies, inspired by
LinkedIn and Wuzzuf.
•
Built a responsive frontend using React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS, and implemented a secure
backend with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB, including authentication and RESTful APIs.
Social Network program
•
Developed a Java-based application with a graphical user interface (GUI) for manipulating and
analyzing XML files representing users in a social network.
•
Implemented features: File Handling, Error Correction, Formatting, Compression, Undo/Redo and
Network Analysis.

Art Shop Website
•
Developed a dynamic and interactive Art Shop website using the Python Django framework, following
a three-tier architecture model.
•
Integrated essential features such as product listings, user authentication, shopping cart functionality,
and secure checkout process.
Door Locker Security Systems
•
Developing a system to unlock a door using a password, the project is implemented based on the
layered architecture model, using two ATmega32 Microcontrollers HMI_ECU (Human Machine
Interface) and Control_ECU.
•
Implementing all the needed drivers: Drivers: GPIO, LCD, Keypad, Timer, UART, I2C, EEPROM,
PWM, Buzzer and DC-motor.
Skill
Programming Languages: Java, Python, JavaScript, C/C++, Embedded C
Web: HTML, CSS, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, Node.js, Express
Databases: SQL, MongoDB
Embedded: AVR, Drivers & Peripheral Interfacing, AUTOSAR
Concepts & Tools: Data Structures & Algorithms, OOP, Git, REST APIs, Authentication & Authorization
Experience
Integration Internship provided by Worex Company January 2026 – March 2026
Full Stack Track – National Telecommunication Institute (NTI)
Focused on Backend development with Node.js, Frontend with React, and DevOps concepts, including a
practical training month in a partnering company. October 2025 – February 2026
Embedded Systems Course provided by Siemens Company July 2025 – September 2025
DevOps internship provided by Ministry of Communication and Information Technology.
(Digital Egypt Pioneers Initiative). April 2024 – October 2024
Full Embedded Systems Diploma July 2023 – October 2023
Java Programming Course, on Coursera September 2023
Programming Foundations with JavaScript, HTML and CSS Course, on Coursera
August 2023
General Electrical Engineering Training, at The Arab Contractors July 2022
Employability Skills Training, at ASU Career Center August 2021

sections.heading: Projects
sections.content:
Graduation Project
An Eclipse-based AUTOSAR Tool (Grade: A)
Sponsored by Garraio, LLC — Supervised by Dr. Sherif Hammad
•
Developed a Java-based AUTOSAR authoring tool using ARTOP (AUTOSAR Tool Platform) as a
foundation for model handling and validation.
•
Applied Eclipse Rich Client Platform (RCP) to create a GUI for configuring Atomic SWCs, Interfaces,
Ports, and nested Composition Structures.
•
Enabled the automated generation of ARXML files, as well as RTE-compliant code: RTE.c, RTE.h,
SWCs.c, and SWC.h, following AUTOSAR RTE_SWS specifications.
Other Projects:
JobHuntly – Job Hunting & Recruitment Platform
•
Developed a full-stack recruitment platform connecting job seekers and companies, inspired by
LinkedIn and Wuzzuf.
•
Built a responsive frontend using React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS, and implemented a secure
backend with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB, including authentication and RESTful APIs.
Social Network program
•
Developed a Java-based application with a graphical user interface (GUI) for manipulating and
analyzing XML files representing users in a social network.
•
Implemented features: File Handling, Error Correction, Formatting, Compression, Undo/Redo and
Network Analysis.
Art Shop Website
•
Developed a dynamic and interactive Art Shop website using the Python Django framework, following
a three-tier architecture model.
•
Integrated essential features such as product listings, user authentication, shopping cart functionality,
and secure checkout process.
Door Locker Security Systems
•
Developing a system to unlock a door using a password, the project is implemented based on the
layered architecture model, using two ATmega32 Microcontrollers HMI_ECU (Human Machine
Interface) and Control_ECU.
•
Implementing all the needed drivers: Drivers: GPIO, LCD, Keypad, Timer, UART, I2C, EEPROM,
PWM, Buzzer and DC-motor.
Skill
Programming Languages: Java, Python, JavaScript, C/C++, Embedded C
Web: HTML, CSS, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, Node.js, Express
Databases: SQL, MongoDB
Embedded: AVR, Drivers & Peripheral Interfacing, AUTOSAR
Concepts & Tools: Data Structures & Algorithms, OOP, Git, REST APIs, Authentication & Authorization

full_text:
Objective
- Creative and detail-oriented Social Media Specialist & Content Creator, experienced in managing platforms,
producing engaging content, and fostering strong online communities. Skilled in video editing, visual design, and
leveraging trends to boost engagement across Facebook, YouTube, and TikTok.
Education
Faculty of Business Information Systems ( BIS ) , Helwan University
Sep 2019 - June 2023
- Overall GPA: 3.2 - Graduation project: A+
Work Experience
Content Creator & Teacher Assistant (Ms Eman Wardy) Sep 2019 - April 2024
- Responsible for the technical part, which includes (building a database of students - managing and operating the
teacher's educational platform - responding to all students' inquiries and solving their issues - interactive content creator
for all social platforms Facebook, TikTok and YouTube - turning the teacher into a successful brand - shooting teasers
videos for the educational material - organizing tasks for the work team - managing the organization of trips)

- Grew a Facebook page from 0 to 10,000+ followers organically within 3 years.
- Built a YouTube channel to 1,000+ subscribers and 60K+ views in the first year without paid ads.
- Created, edited, and published daily content leveraging trends to boost engagement.
- Skilled in Facebook, YouTube, TikTok, analytics tools, CapCut, and Canva.
- Designed YouTube thumbnails and social media visuals that improved reach and click-through rates.
- Managed community engagement through timely responses to comments and messages.

Courses
- Banque Masr, Pioneers Online Program , 2021
- The Cybersecurity Threat Landscape , 2025

Technical skills
- Proficient with Microsoft Office (Word , PowerPoint , Excel).
- Social Media Platforms Management (Facebook, YouTube, TikTok) .
- Video Editing (CapCut) .
- Graphic Design (Canva) .
Personal skills
- Communication skills both written and verbal.
- Exceptional attention to detail.
- Advanced organizational skills.
- Teamwork, Leadership, multitasking and problem-solving skills.
- Proven ability to implement process improvement initiatives.
- Emotional Intelligence and Critical Thinking Skills.
- Research and thrive under pressure.
- Fast learner To Master.

Languages
- English: very good
- Arabic: Native
Giza , Egypt
+(20)1220856170
MarƟnnathan12345@gmail.com
MarƟn Nathan

full_text:
Mazen Hassan Elsayad
DevOps Engineer
Tanta, Egypt | 01286168041 | mazenelsayad99@gmail.com
https://www.linkedin.com/in/mazen-hassan22 | https://github.com/mazenhassan20
Summary:
Motivated communication engineering student with a passion for DevOps and cloud
technologies, looking to build practical skills and contribute to real projects , seeking an
internship to gain hands-on experience and grow within a collaborative, professional team.
Education:
- Tanta University, Electronics and Electrical Communications Engineering Department
Grade: Good 2021-2026
Experience:
Digital Egypt Pioneers (DEPI) – DevOps Intern 07/2025-12/2025
Currently enrolled in the DevOps track, gaining skills in cloud technologies, automation, and
CI/CD pipelines. Continuing to build practical experience through ongoing training.
NTI - Linux Red Hat Administration Track 07/2025-08/2025
trained on Linux system administration, covering Red Hat fundamentals, command-line
skills, user and permission management, and server configurations.
Intern at WE – CCNA v7 08/2024 -09/2024
Learned networking basics like TCP/IP and OSI, with hands-on routing, switching, and
security. Practiced IP addressing, network services, and got familiar with WAN technologies.
ITI 1 month training - Developing web application using python 07/2024 -08/2024
Built a foundation in HTML, CSS, and the basics of PostgreSQL and databases, while
improving my core Python skills. I practiced OOP concepts, data structures, and file
handling, gaining confidence in writing organized, functional code for web applications.
Projects:
Automated DevOps Cloud Infrastructure (DEPI Final Project): Designed and deployed a
scalable, production-ready infrastructure on AWS using Terraform (IaC) and EKS.
Implemented a full GitOps workflow utilizing ArgoCD for automated Kubernetes
deployments, integrated with a robust CI pipeline via Jenkins and GitHub Actions. Ensured
code quality via SonarQube and established real-time observability using Prometheus and
Grafana. Tools: AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes (EKS),Minikube, Docker, Jenkins, ArgoCD,
Nexus, Prometheus, Grafana.

AKS-Project: End-to-End GitOps on Azure
Provisioned a scalable Azure environment using Terraform, automated CI/CD with
GitHub Actions and ArgoCD for GitOps-driven deployments, and managed a Node.js/React
stack using Helm, Nginx Ingress, and Prometheus/Grafana for full observability.

Technical skills:
• Network concepts and protocols
• Linux
• Windows Server Administration
• Virtualization(VMware)
• Docker
• Kubernetes
• ansible
• Git&github
• Jenkins
• GitHub actions
• terraform
• Prometheus & Grafana
• Cloud basics
• python

Soft skills:

• Time management
• Presentation skills
• Problem solving
• Self-learning
• Collaboration
Volunteering:
Media Member at IEEE Tanta 02/2025-present
Contribute to media coverage by designing posts, capturing event photos and videos, and
supporting the chapter’s visual identity.
PR Member at Enactus & Rally 10/2024-10/2025
Handle public relations tasks including contacting partners, promoting events, and helping
manage participant communication and outreach.
Social media member at YLY Tanta 08/2024- 08/2025
Volunteer creating social media designs, writing posts, and documenting events with photos
and videos. Also support organizing and archiving event materials.

Languages:
• Arabic, mother tongue.
• English, good written and spoken.

Profile
• Backend Java Developer with practical experience building REST APIs and small services using Java (11+),
Spring Boot and Hibernate. Delivered production-like features, wrote unit tests (JUnit), and migrated legacy
CLI tools to Spring services. Strong OOP foundation; motivated to deepen cloud-native and JVM
performance skills.:
Education
• Modern Academy
Cairo, Egypt
Bachelor of Engineering - Computer Engineering; Grade: Good
September 2019 - June 2024
Courses: Operating Systems, Data Structures, Computer Architecture, Artificial Intelligence, Microcontrollers, Networking, Databases
Projects
• Role-based Employee CRUD Application: Developed a secure web dashboard that displays employees in a dynamic table
with options to add, update, partially update, and delete records. Integrated role-based admin permissions controlling which
CRUD operations each admin can perform. Used BCrypt for password hashing and Spring Data JPA for persistence. Tech:
Java 11+, Spring, Spring REST, Hibernate, Spring Security, Thymeleaf, IntelliJ, Maven, MySQL, Git
• CRM Car Booking System - Spring Boot REST Services: Migrated a Java CLI booking application to a Spring Boot
REST service, designing domain models (User, Car, Booking), DAOs and service layers. Implemented endpoints for booking
lifecycle (create, update, cancel, list) and added JUnit tests to cover core business logic. Improved code maintainability by
introducing dependency injection, modular DAO interfaces, and Streams-based filtering. Tech: Java 11+, Spring, Spring
REST, Hibernate, Maven, IntelliJ, MySQL, JUnit, Git
• Face Recognition System - Graduation Project: Developed an end-to-end face recognition system: created and labeled a
face dataset, used MTCNN for detection, extracted FaceNet embeddings, and trained an SVM recognizer in Python/OpenCV
— integrated into an Anaconda environment and Flask. Tech: Python, Anaconda, Pandas, Scikit, Flask, OpenCV, FaceNet

Cairo, Egypt
minaphilips98@gmail.com 01154616820
Mina Tharwat MinaTharwat93
PROFILE
Data Scientist specializing in Deep Learning and Machine Learning Deployment (Streamlit, TFLite). Certified professional
with strong command of Python and SQL. Demonstrated success in building high-impact solutions, including a 95%
accurate skin-burn classifier (EfficientNetB3) and an 85% accurate deployed heart-disease prediction model.
EDUCATION
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science | Helwan University, Cairo | 2021 – 2025
•
Graduation Project: Skin Burn Classification
Data Scientist Professional Diploma | Epsilon AI | Dec 2023 – Jan 2025
•
Developed ML models (regression & classification) with Scikit-learn/TensorFlow (up to 90% accuracy).
•
Conducted EDA and preprocessing using Pandas & NumPy to enhance data quality.
•
Utilized SQL for data handling and deployed interactive Streamlit apps.
EXPERIENCE
Machine Learning Internship | NeuronetiX | Aug 2024 – Sep 2024
•
Built supervised and unsupervised models with Scikit-learn, improving model performance by 10% through feature
engineering.
•
Cleaned and preprocessed datasets to ensure high-quality inputs for machine learning pipelines.
•
Collaborated with teams to analyze model performance and present actionable insights.
CERTIFICATIONS
•
Certified Data Scientist Professional, Epsilon AI (2025)
•
Sprints x Microsoft Summer Camp - AI and Machine Learning (2025)
•
SprintUp - AI and Machine Learning Foundations (2025)
•
DataCamp – Intermediate SQL (2025)
•
DataCamp – Introduction to SQL (2025)
•
Machine Learning Internship, NeuronetiX (2024)
•
Google Coursera: Ask Questions to Make Data-Driven Decisions (2023)
•
Google Coursera: Foundations: Data, Data, Everywhere (2023)
SKILLS
•
Technical Skills:
Programming: Python, SQL, Pandas, NumPy
Machine Learning: Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, Keras, Supervised/Unsupervised Learning, Feature Engineering
Visualization: Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly
Tools: Git, GitHub, Streamlit, MySQL, Jupyter Notebook, VS Code

•
Soft Skills: Problem-Solving, Critical Thinking, Analytical Thinking, Communication, Teamwork, Adaptability, Fast
Learner, Time Management, Collaboration, Attention to Detail

PROJECTS
Skin Burn Classification
Description: Built a deep learning model with EfficientNetB3 to classify skin burn images into 4 severity classes,
achieving 95% accuracy—converted model to TFLite for efficient deployment on resource-constrained devices.

Heart Disease Classification

Summary
Innovative Machine Learning Engineer skilled in building intelligent data-driven solutions, combining
analytical precision with creative problem-solving to deliver real business impact. Passionate about
transforming complex data into actionable insights through AI, deep learning, and advanced modelling.
Experience
Machine Learning Intern — Elevvo Pathways [10 Aug 2025 – 10 Sep 2025]
Delivered high-impact ML projects independently, showcasing strong analytical thinking, model
optimization expertise, and a data-driven problem-solving mindset.
Machine Learning Intern — Genius Technology Center (GTC) [30 Aug 2025 – 26 Sep 2025]
Collaborated with a cross-functional team to design and deploy real-world ML models using
TensorFlow and Scikit-learn, driving data insights into measurable business value.
PROJECTS
Diabetes Prediction System – Machine Learning Project
Developed and deployed a supervised machine learning system for diabetes prediction using
100,000+ real patient records, performing full data preprocessing (missing value handling,
encoding, scaling), applying SMOTETomek for class balancing and GridSearchCV for tuning, and
selecting Random Forest as the final model with 95.4% accuracy and strong recall performance,
delivered via a Streamlit web application for real-time prediction.
GitHub Repository

Fayoum University

Mohamed Ahmed Mounir
New Cairo
mohamedahmedmounir2@gmail.com | +20121 2430324
GitHub
A driven 18-year-old student pursuing a bachelor's degree in computer science, I have participated
in multiple robotics competitions and served as a physics teacher assistant. In addition to winning
several medals in swimming, I have interned multiple times at Hamleys Toy Store, gaining valuable
experience in customer service and retail operations. As a wheelchair user, I embrace challenges
and demonstrate resilience in both my academic and athletic pursuits.

Experiences
The University of Hertfordshire
10/24 - Present
External Relations Member Student Union

sponsors to support Student Union initiatives.

Talent Show, Cultural Festival, and concerts, contributing to increased funding and visibility.
• Coordinated and negotiated sponsorship agreements, ensuring mutual value and effective
brand representation.
• Represented the Student Union in meetings and communications with external stakeholders
to promote collaboration and student engagement.
• Supported event planning teams by facilitating external support, marketing coordination,
and logistics with partners.

10/24 – 01/26

world community challenges.

stakeholders.

progress and impact measurement.
• Promoted Enactus values through campus engagement and awareness activities, fostering a
culture of social responsibility.
IGCSE Physics teacher assistant 09/23 – 08/25

•

Contact parents with updates on student progress and achievements.

New capital Model United Nations 2024
Delegate
Participated in Model United Nations, specifically in the International Court of Justice (ICJ)
simulation, where I developed skills in legal argumentation and international law. Engaged in
detailed research and mock trials, honing my abilities in legal writing and oral advocacy.
• Represented a country in the International Court of Justice (ICJ) simulation at Model United
Nations.
• Drafted legal arguments and participated in mock trials to resolve international disputes.
• Developed a deep understanding of international law and court procedures.
• Engaged in rigorous research and analysis to support legal claims and counterclaims.
• Demonstrated proficiency in legal writing and oral advocacy, earning recognition for
excellence in ICJ sessions.

Mersal NPO volunteering training 2022
Trainee
• Assist in organizing and executing community outreach programs and events.
• Support daily operations by handling administrative tasks and data management.
• Help with the planning and implementation of various projects aimed at community
development.
• ' Engage with community members to provide support and gather feedback.

Hamleys Toy Store August -2019 & August -2021
Intern
• Assisted customers with inquiries and provided product recommendations to enhance their
shopping experience.
• Handled transactions and processed sales efficiently at the checkout.
• Supported inventory management by monitoring stock levels and restocking shelves as
needed. ' Collaborated with team members to ensure a smooth and enjoyable shopping
environment.

Projects

Student Record Management System | Python

•
Developed a modular console-based Student Record Management System using
Python.
•
Implemented dynamic GPA calculation (0–4.5 scale) with automated academic
classification logic.
•
Designed per-module pass/fail evaluation with handling for new and returning
students.
•
Built robust input validation (unique ID, unique 12-digit phone number, age and
grade constraints).
•
Integrated JSON-based data persistence for structured storage and retrieval.
•
Implemented statistical reporting (average GPA, classification distribution, top
student per major).
•
Developed automated export functionality for structured academic report
generation.
•
Applied core Python concepts including functions, loops, conditionals, dictionaries,
file I/O, and validation.

⸻

Hospital Management System Database | Oracle SQL

Tools: Oracle SQL, ERD Plus

•
Designed a complete ERD for a Hospital Management System using ERD Plus.
•
Modelled core entities: Patient, Staff, Department, Ward, Room, Admission,
Treatment, Test, Invoice.
•
Defined primary and foreign key relationships to ensure data integrity.
•
Implemented fully normalized relational schema in Oracle SQL (up to 3NF).
•
Inserted structured sample data (10+ records per table) to simulate operations.
•
Developed advanced SQL queries using JOIN, GROUP BY, COUNT, SUM, ORDER BY,
and data manipulation commands.
•
Applied database design principles including 1:N and 1:1 relationships and
mandatory/optional constraints.
•
Demonstrated strong understanding of relational database architecture and
healthcare data modeling.

⸻

SIM-8 Custom 8-bit Microcontroller | Logisim-Evolution

• Designed and implemented a complete 8-bit microcontroller architecture using
Logisim-Evolution.
• Built 12 modular subcircuits including PC, MAR, MDR, IR, Register File (R0–R3), ALU,
RES, SR, RAM (256×8), I/O ports, Writeback MUX, and Control Unit (FSM).
• Implemented full fetch–decode–execute cycle using a Finite State Machine control
unit.
• Designed an 8-bit ALU supporting arithmetic and logic operations (ADD, SUB, AND,
OR, XOR, NOT, SHL, SHR) with flag generation (Z, N, C, V).
• Developed a 4-register file with dual-read and single-write architecture.
• Integrated 256×8 RAM with memory read/write control and instruction storage.
• Implemented multi-input writeback MUX for data selection (ALU, memory, I/O).
• Designed status register for real-time flag updates and conditional execution.
• Simulated and tested assembly programs including LOAD, ADD, STORE, OUT, and
HALT.
• Applied structured bus architecture (data, address, control) for scalable CPU design.

Education

pages.text:
Projects

Student Record Management System | Python

•
Developed a modular console-based Student Record Management System using
Python.
•
Implemented dynamic GPA calculation (0–4.5 scale) with automated academic
classification logic.
•
Designed per-module pass/fail evaluation with handling for new and returning
students.
•
Built robust input validation (unique ID, unique 12-digit phone number, age and
grade constraints).
•
Integrated JSON-based data persistence for structured storage and retrieval.
•
Implemented statistical reporting (average GPA, classification distribution, top
student per major).
•
Developed automated export functionality for structured academic report
generation.
•
Applied core Python concepts including functions, loops, conditionals, dictionaries,
file I/O, and validation.

⸻

Hospital Management System Database | Oracle SQL

Tools: Oracle SQL, ERD Plus

•
Designed a complete ERD for a Hospital Management System using ERD Plus.
•
Modelled core entities: Patient, Staff, Department, Ward, Room, Admission,
Treatment, Test, Invoice.
•
Defined primary and foreign key relationships to ensure data integrity.
•
Implemented fully normalized relational schema in Oracle SQL (up to 3NF).
•
Inserted structured sample data (10+ records per table) to simulate operations.
•
Developed advanced SQL queries using JOIN, GROUP BY, COUNT, SUM, ORDER BY,
and data manipulation commands.
•
Applied database design principles including 1:N and 1:1 relationships and
mandatory/optional constraints.
•
Demonstrated strong understanding of relational database architecture and
healthcare data modeling.

pages.text:
⸻

SIM-8 Custom 8-bit Microcontroller | Logisim-Evolution

• Designed and implemented a complete 8-bit microcontroller architecture using
Logisim-Evolution.
• Built 12 modular subcircuits including PC, MAR, MDR, IR, Register File (R0–R3), ALU,
RES, SR, RAM (256×8), I/O ports, Writeback MUX, and Control Unit (FSM).
• Implemented full fetch–decode–execute cycle using a Finite State Machine control
unit.
• Designed an 8-bit ALU supporting arithmetic and logic operations (ADD, SUB, AND,
OR, XOR, NOT, SHL, SHR) with flag generation (Z, N, C, V).
• Developed a 4-register file with dual-read and single-write architecture.
• Integrated 256×8 RAM with memory read/write control and instruction storage.
• Implemented multi-input writeback MUX for data selection (ALU, memory, I/O).
• Designed status register for real-time flag updates and conditional execution.
• Simulated and tested assembly programs including LOAD, ADD, STORE, OUT, and
HALT.
• Applied structured bus architecture (data, address, control) for scalable CPU design.

full_text:
MOHAMED SABER
SUMMARY
Computer Science senior at Ain Shams University with hands-on experience in full-stack software
development using React, TypeScript, Node.js, and Express. Strong understanding of RESTful APIs,
authentication systems, modular architecture, and database design. Familiar with Jest-based unit
testing and AWS cloud environments (AWS SAA-C03 certified). Experienced in Agile/SCRUM
environments, writing maintainable code, fixing defects, implementing small feature enhancements,
and collaborating through Git workflows. Passionate about building scalable, secure, and
production-ready software solutions.
PROJECTS
Developed full-stack application using React, TypeScript, Node.js, and MongoDB.
Designed RESTful API endpoints for AI prompt handling and database operations.
Integrated external AI models for intelligent plan generation.
Applied clean code principles and modular design patterns.
Performed debugging, testing, and documentation updates.
Used Git for version control and collaborative development.
Ai Fitness Trainer
Mondeux (Jewelery Brand)
Designed and developed RESTful APIs using Node.js and Express.
Implemented authentication and authorization using JWT and role-based access control.
Built modular backend architecture to support scalability and maintainability.
Fixed bugs and optimized MongoDB queries, achieving <100ms response time.
Developed responsive frontend components using React and TypeScript.
Participated in feature enhancements and iterative development using Git version control.
Tested API endpoints and validated data flows using Postman.
Cairo, Egypt • +201202771936 • mohamedsaberr@outlook.sa
• github.com/mohameddsaber • linkedin.com/in/xmohamedsaber

Frontend Development: React.js, Next.js, TailwindCSS
Backend Development: Node.js, Express.js
Databases: MongoDB, SQL, Redis
Testing & Tools: Jest (unit testing), Postman, Git, MongoDB Atlas
Cloud & DevOps: AWS (SAA-C03 certified), environment configuration & deployment support
Concepts: RESTful API Development, Authentication (JWT, OAuth), Role-Based Access Control,
Clean Architecture, Modular Design, State Management, Debugging, Performance Optimization
TECHNICAL SKILLS
Full-stack web application that analyzes resumes, optimizes content for Applicant Tracking
Systems (ATS), and generates developer portfolios.
Designed and developed RESTful APIs using Node.js and Express.
Built dynamic frontend using React and TypeScript with reusable components.
Implemented resume parsing and structured data extraction logic.
Developed CV keyword optimization logic aligned with ATS requirements.
Designed modular backend architecture for scalability and maintainability.
Integrated authentication and user session management.
Applied clean architecture principles and Git-based workflow for iterative feature
development.
Focused on performance, structured validation, and production-ready code quality.

- Enhanced UI/UX and core features in Al-Muslim and Azkar Al-Muslim, improving usability and
Use retention.Contributed to feature updates in Salli Ala Al-Nabi (1M+ downloads), increasing

solving, and the full development lifecycle—from UI/UX to post-release improvements—ensuring
high-quality, on-time delivery.

- Developed multiple applications: Chat App, FlickBox, CleverQuest, and Hospital System
- Applied Java, Kotlin, OOP, SOLID principles, and design patterns in real projects
- Built features using Dagger Hilt, MVVM, MVC, MVP, and Firebase
- Integrated REST APIs with Retrofit, reducing network failures by 20% via optimized error handling.
- Gained hands-on experience with data structures and clean architecture

- Implemented layouts with LazyRow and LazyColumn for efficient state handling and performance

Stock Trading MVC - ASP.NET Core
Developed a production-ready MVC using ASP.NET Core following Clean Architecture principles. Designed a
multi-layered architecture with clear separation of concerns (Entities, Services, Service Contracts). Implemented
Dependency Injection, DTO pattern, and service abstractions for maintainability and testability. Integrated Finnhub
external API to fetch real-time stock data using HttpClient Factory. Built a comprehensive validation system using
custom validation attributes and data annotations. Wrote 20+ unit tests using xUnit covering business logic, edge
cases, and async methods. Applied async/​await and structured exception handling to ensure scalability and
reliability.
E-Commerce Web API - ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework Core
Designed a full enterprise-grade Web API using Clean Architecture.
Implemented CQRS with MediatR for commands and queries.
Built a complete authentication system using Identity & JWT.
Applied Repository Pattern, Unit of Work, EF Core configurations, and ULID keys.
Implemented cart, wishlist, products, and orders modules following domain-driven principles.

full_text:
Mohamed Nasser Sheikhon
Cairo, Nasr City, Zahraa Madinet Nasr | +201022752938 | LinkedIn
GitHub | mohamednasserr654@gmail.com

OBJECTIVE
Backend Developer with a strong foundations in computer science and practical experience in building
applications using ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, and SQL Server. Skilled in designing scalable APIs,
integrating databases, and delivering efficient backend solutions. Passionate about backend architecture,
performance optimization, and contributing to impactful software projects.

EDUCATION
Ain Shams University – Faculty of Computer and Information Science
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science (Major: Computer Science)
Expected Graduation: July 2026
CGPA: 3.4

INTERNSHIP EXPERIENCE
EJADA Egypt – Backend (Offshore Support & Demand Center) Intern
Ejada, Almazah, Cairo Aug 2025 – Sep 2025

Developed and maintained backend services and web applications using Java, Spring Boot, Spring Security, and
RESTful APIs, ensuring secure and scalable system design.

Leveraged JPA, Hibernate, and Microservices architecture to build scalable, high-performance backend modules
with efficient data handling and persistence.
Information Technology Institute (ITI) – Web Development using ASP.NET Core MVC Intern
Online Sep 2025 – Oct 2025

Completed a practical internship focused on ASP.NET Core MVC Web Development using C#, Entity Framework
Core, and SQL Server.

Gained hands-on experience in building dynamic, data-driven web applications by applying concepts of MVC
architecture, dependency injection, database design, and authentication & authorization.

Applied SOLID principles, clean code practices, and layered architecture to ensure scalability, maintainability,
and high-quality software design.

TRAINING & COURSES
Ain Shams University
Android Development Intern (Summer Training) Aug 2025 – Sep 2025
Built a mobile application using Java and Android SDK as part of the training program.
Web Development Intern (Summer Training) Aug 2024 – Sep 2024
Developed a web project using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript as part of the training program.

PROJECTS
 Map Routing Project (C#): Applied graph algorithms (shortest path) with a focus on time complexity
optimization for efficient route calculation.
 Pac-Man Game (C++): Built using Graph and SFML, applying data structures and BFS algorithm, with
basic AI techniques for enemy behavior.
 Operating System (C): Designed and developed operating system modules covering memory
management, process scheduling, and concurrency control with locks.
 Library Management System (Java): Developed using Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) principles to
manage books, users, and transactions.
 Flight Reservation DBMS Project (SQL): Developed a relational database system to handle flight
bookings, passenger information, and scheduling.
 Gym Website (HTML , CSS , JS): Developed a responsive website showcasing gym services and
membership features.
 Sentiment Analysis Classification (Python): Implemented using Natural Language Processing (NLP)
techniques to classify text based on sentiment.
 Connect-4 Game (C++): Implemented game logic, win detection algorithms, and integrated audio
effects for an enhanced gameplay experience.
 Video Game Sales Prediction (Python): Applied Machine Learning algorithms to analyze datasets and
predict video game sales based on features such as genre, platform, and region.
 Tiny Compiler (C#): Developed a compiler for the Tiny language in C#, including lexical analysis, parsing,
and code generation.
 E-Commerce Website (Java, Spring Boot, Microservices): Developed the backend of an e-commerce
platform, implementing REST APIs, product and order management, and a scalable microservices
architecture.
 University Management System (ASP.NET Core MVC): Enabled the management of students,
instructors, and courses with defined roles and access levels for each user type. Implemented key
features such as user registration and login, CRUD operations, role-based access control, and data
management using Entity Framework Core.

SKILLS
Programming & Frameworks: C++, C#, Java, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework, Spring Boot, Microservices,
Python
Web Technologies: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL
Core Concepts: REST APIs, OOP, Data Structures, Algorithms, Problem Solving
Soft Skills: Teamwork, Leadership, Time Management, Networking Fundamentals

LANGUAGES
Arabic (Native)
English (Very good)

full_text:
Mostafa Saleh
Cyber Security Analyst
First Settlement, New Cairo, Egypt
 +20 101 603 4783
# mostafa2002.ms@gmail.com
ï Mostafa Saleh
Summary
Cybersecurity graduate with hands-on SOC training and practical experience in incident response, threat detection,
and security monitoring. Skilled in SIEM log analysis, network traffic investigation, and vulnerability assessment
using tools such as Splunk, ELK Stack, and Wireshark. Ranked in the Top 4% globally on TryHackMe.
Education
Misr International University
2020 – 2024
B.Sc. in Computer Science (Cyber Security)
Cairo, Egypt
– Overall Grade: Very Good (GPA: 2.71)
– Graduation Project Grade: Excellent (GPA: 3.7)
Experience
Petrojet
July 2023 – August 2023
Information Technology Intern
Egypt
– Assisted in configuring, maintaining, and troubleshooting computer systems and network devices.
– Supported the IT team in diagnosing hardware and software issues across multiple departments.
– Helped monitor network performance and ensured stable connectivity for internal users.
– Provided technical support for installing and updating operating systems and essential applications.
– Documented IT processes, troubleshooting steps, and system configurations for future reference.
Certifications & Training
SOC Level 1 Learning Path | TryHackMe
Jan 2026 – March 2026
– Completed hands-on SOC analyst training covering log analysis, SIEM usage, incident investigation, and
threat detection.
– Practiced analyzing Windows & Linux logs, detecting brute-force attacks, phishing attempts, and malware
activity.
– Worked with tools including Splunk, ELK Stack, and Wireshark in simulated SOC environments.
– Investigated real-world attack scenarios including lateral movement, privilege escalation, and C2 detection.
– Performed threat hunting and IOC analysis in simulated enterprise environments.
– Ranked in the Top 4% globally on TryHackMe platform.
eCIR (Certified Incident Responder) Preparation | Netriders Academy
Dec 2025 – Jan 2026
– Hands-on training in Incident Handling & Incident Response aligned with SOC operations and Blue Team
methodologies.
– Performed network traffic and packet analysis using Wireshark to identify malicious activity, C2 traffic, and
anomalies.
– Conducted event and log analysis using SIEM platforms (Splunk & ELK Stack) for threat detection and
correlation.
– Investigated process behavior, indicators of compromise (IOCs), and anomalies during simulated incident
scenarios.
CCNA | Cisco
Nov 2023 – Jan 2024
– Configured small Cisco networks including VLANs, trunking, and single-area OSPF routing.
– Implemented core IP services such as NAT, DHCP, and NTP with basic security controls.
– Applied ACLs, port security, and device hardening with troubleshooting using standard tools.

Projects
Web Vulnerability Scanner | Python (Graduation Project)
2023 – 2024
– Developed an automated web vulnerability scanner targeting SQL Injection, XSS, RCE, and port scanning.
– Implemented payload testing using time- and boolean-based SQLi and reflected/stored XSS checks.
– Generated prioritized security reports with severity levels and remediation guidance.
– Applied secure coding practices to prevent introducing new vulnerabilities.
– Led team tasks and contributed technical solutions throughout the project lifecycle.
Skills
Programming & Scripting:
Python, SQL, C, Java
SIEM & Log Analysis:
Splunk, ELK Stack, IBM QRadar
Network & Traffic Analysis:
Wireshark
Security Tools:
Nessus
Operating Systems:
Linux, Windows
Soft Skills:
Problem Solving, Analytical Thinking, Communication, Teamwork, Adaptability

Nada Mohamed Omar Aboelsaad
Cairo, Egypt | +201277946126 | omarnada037@gmail.com
LinkedIn: nada-aboelsaad-0323b7273
Education
Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Communications Engineering
09/2021 – 02/2026
Egypt-Japan University of Science and Technology (E-JUST)
• CGPA: 3.86/4.0
Academic Scholarship
• Graduation Project: Design and Implementation of a LoRa Transceiver System
• Supervisors: Prof. Maha Elsabrouty, Dr. Sameh Sherif
Engineering Experience
Teaching Assistant, Electronics and Communications Engineering Department
Egypt-Japan University of Science and Technology (E-JUST)
Feb 2026 – Present
• Deliver tutorials and supervise laboratory sessions for undergraduate students.
• Courses taught: Electronics Engineering, Signals and Systems, Digital Signal Processing.
Mobile Networks Intern
08/2025 – 09/2025
Nokia
• Completed a 4-week intensive internship focused on LTE and 5G network architecture, optimization, and deployment
strategies.
• Gained hands-on understanding of how LTE/5G network performance can be enhanced through KPI analysis, SON au-
tomation, and AI-driven optimization.
• Utilized Power BI and AI-based analytics for digital twin simulations and network planning assessment.
• Collaborated on developing a conceptual project: a Smart Stadium in Egypt using 5G NSA for IoT crowd management
and high-speed connectivity.
• Studied ESG principles, microwave link planning, and AI integration in next-generation network operations.
Huawei Network Administrator Course
07/2025 – 12/2025
DEPI
• Completed 100+ hours of HCIA/HCIP training, mastering routing and switching configurations.
• Configured Huawei devices in simulated enterprise networks, reducing setup errors.
• Implemented robust network security measures for scalable infrastructure.
Instrumentation and Control Intern
08/2024 – 09/2024
Giza Systems
• Calibrated Honeywell equipment (pressure, temperature, level, flow) with 98% accuracy.
• Applied Fire and Gas sector knowledge (IR, electrochemical) and Dr¨ager maintenance.
• Performed splicing, OTDR, and VNA measurements, improving system diagnostics.
• Investigated image processing for flame detectors to enhance reliability.
Fiber Optics Transmission Intern
07/2023 – 08/2023
WE Telecom
• Assisted in installation, testing, and maintenance of fiber optic transmission systems.
• Performed splicing, OTDR testing, and troubleshooting of optical links.
• Studied DWDM/SDH technologies, optical budgeting, and link evaluation.
• Ensured network reliability within the national fiber backbone.
Technical Skills
Programming: Python, C++, C, MATLAB, Verilog, Software Testing
Electronics Competencies: Embedded Systems, Fiber Optics, PCB Design, FPGA Development
Networking Abilities: Network Administration
Languages: English (C1), French (B2), Arabic (Native)
1

Technical Projects
LoRa Communication System on FPGA (Graduation Project)
02/2025 – 02/2026
• Designed and implemented a complete LoRa transceiver architecture on FPGA to support low-power, long-range IoT
communication.
• Developed Verilog modules for chirp modulation, dechirping, symbol synchronization, and preamble detection.
• Conducted extensive simulations and on-board testing to evaluate performance under various channel conditions.
• Demonstrated a functional hardware spread-spectrum communication system aligned with modern IoT applications.
Multi-Site Enterprise Network
12/2025
• Built a full enterprise network interconnecting multiple branches using an MPLS Layer-3 VPN architecture.
• Configured core network services including BGP, OSPF, VLAN segmentation, Inter-VLAN routing, DHCP, NAT, and
ACLs.
• Ensured redundancy and high availability using VRRP, EtherChannel, and STP, followed by full connectivity testing.
Traffic Light Control System on FPGA
09/2024 – 01/2025
• Designed and implemented an adaptive traffic light controller using Verilog on an Altera Cyclone III FPGA.
• Reduced intersection congestion by 20% using dynamic timing algorithms and real-time signal coordination.
Antenna Design and Fabrication
09/2024 – 01/2025
• Designed, simulated, and fabricated a high-efficiency 2.4 GHz microstrip patch antenna for Wi-Fi applications.
• Achieved approximately 95% radiation efficiency verified through electromagnetic simulation and field testing.
Voice-to-Sign Language Translation System
03/2024 – 07/2024
• Developed a robotic-hand-based translation system achieving 95% accurate speech-to-gesture recognition.
• Integrated speech processing, microcontroller control, and robotic actuation to deliver low-latency response.
Smart Farming System
09/2023 – 01/2024
• Built a computer-vision-based agricultural monitoring tool capable of automated apple counting with 90% accuracy.
• Improved farming efficiency through real-time detection and data-driven insights for crop monitoring.
Publications
Amera Sharkawy, Omayma Elshazly, Mostafa S. Nada, Nada Mohamed Omar Aboelsaad, Ahd Hesham, Maha Elsabrouty,
and Sameh Sherif, “Design and Implementation of a LoRa Modulator on FPGA”, in 2025 International Japan-Africa
Conference on Electronics, Communications, and Computations (JAC-ECC), Alexandria, Egypt, Dec. 2025, pp. TBD. doi:
TBD. Published at IEEE Xplore.
Professional Certifications
IELTS Certification: band score 7
01/2026
Huawei Network Administration - DEPI
12/2025
B2 French Language Certification
05/2023
2

full_text:
NOURAN KAMHA
New Cairo, Cairo, Egypt
Phone: +20 1063401133
Email: nourankamha@gmail.com
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/nourankamha
Objective
Motivated and detail-oriented Computer Science and Engineering student at the German University in Cairo,
seeking internship opportunities in software development or full stack engineering. Passionate about building
scalable applications and solving real-world problems using a blend of front-end and back-end technologies. Eager
to contribute to a dynamic tech team while gaining hands-on industry experience.
Experience
Web Developer (Part-Time) – N’s Websites
Jan 2025 – Present
WordPress Website Developer
Remote / Cairo, Egypt
• Designed, built, and maintained responsive websites using WordPress, leveraging themes such as Astra and plugins like
Spectra.
• Customized layouts and UI components using page builders; integrated interactive features such as video sliders and
dynamic sidebars.
• Optimized website performance for page speed, SEO, and WCAG accessibility standards.
• Collaborated within an Agile team using sprints to deliver features, resolve issues, and iterate on client feedback.
Klickit
July 2025 – Sep 2025
Software Engineering Intern – Technology Department
Cairo, Egypt
• Built a real client-facing web platform using .NET (Clean Architecture) and Angular, contributing to a project that
will be deployed in production.
• Applied the CQRS design pattern to improve system structure, maintainability, and scalability.
• Developed and executed effective unit tests to ensure code quality and reliability.
• Implemented secure authentication flows using JWT tokens.
• Collaborated on GitLab, contributing to both frontend and backend tasks while maintaining clear communication to
ensure smooth integration.
• Built a real estate payment platform for developers and prepared a demo for client presentation; additionally extended
KlickIt’s education payment system with an e-commerce module for purchasing school uniforms and books.
Elsewedy Electric
July 2024 – Sep 2024
Full Stack Developer Intern – Application Development and BI Department
Cairo, Egypt
• Developed an Inventory Management System using ASP.NET Core MVC & Entity Framework for the backend
and HTML, JavaScript, CSS, jQuery for the frontend.
• Monitored stock levels across various warehouses, notifying personnel when stock was low.
• Managed inventory by enabling the addition, removal, and transfer of products between stores.
• Generated reports on stock levels, product movements, and trends to support data-driven decision-making.
Education
German University in Cairo
Oct. 2021 – Oct 2026
Faculty of Media Engineering and Technology
Cairo, Egypt
Major: Computer Science and Engineering Program
Projects
Bachelor Project – VR Intelligent Assistant with Arabic-English Code-Switching
Grade: Excellent
Mar 2025 – Jun 2025
• Bachelor thesis project focused on building a virtual reality-based intelligent assistant capable of understanding and
responding to code-switched Arabic-English conversations, particularly Egyptian Arabic.
• Designing and integrating key components including speech-to-text (STT), natural language understanding and
generation, text-to-speech (TTS), and virtual environment interaction.
• Exploring publicly available datasets and constructing custom datasets to support dialectal and context-aware training
and evaluation.
• Targeting use cases in education and tutoring by enabling realistic, responsive, and dialect-aware spoken interaction
within immersive VR settings.

Implementing and designing a game | Java (Eclipse IDE), HTML, CSS, Javascript
May 2023
• The Last of Us: Legacy is a single player survival game set in a zombie apocalyptic world.
• Implemented object-oriented programming practices such as inheritance to create different characters.
Advising System for advising students | SQL Visual Studio Code, HTML, CSS, Javascript
Dec 2023
• Created an advising system where different types of users can use the system. Users are either: Admin, Advisor or
Student.
• The system keeps track of the courses missed, courses selected by students, selected courses’ grade and semester,
students’ GPA, graduation plans.
Non-Monetary Donation Web Application | HTML, CSS, Javascript, Github
Dec 2023
• Non-monetary donation application for an NGO (non-government organization) that will help both the donors and
anyone who is requesting a donation to reach each other.
• The project was divided to two milestones where the first milestone was focusing on collecting requirements engineering,
and the second milestone was about front-end design and development.
• The following points where covered in this project; Learnability, Visibility, Efficiency, Design for errors, Navigation from
page to page and navigation reversibility, Responsiveness of the UI, Consistency of the IU elements.
TravelM8 | MERN Stack (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js)
Nov 2024
• Developed ”TravelM8,” a comprehensive travel planning web application that streamlines vacation planning by
integrating personalized suggestions, real-time notifications, and seamless booking capabilities.
• Implemented key features such as budget-based activity recommendations, curated local attractions, and customizable
itineraries.
• Utilized Agile methodology to deliver the project in 3 sprints, meeting functional and non-functional requirements.
• Contributed to a responsive front-end and scalable back-end, ensuring smooth interaction between all components.
• Collaborated on GitHub, showcasing individual contributions and team effort.
• Link: https://github.com/Advanced-computer-lab-2024/TravelM8
Relevant Coursework
• Data Structures
• Computer Organization
• OOP
• Embedded Systems
• Digital System Design
• Theory of Computation
• Computer Architecture
• Operating System
• Software Engineering
• Databases
• Computer Graphics
• Management
Technical Skills
Languages & Frameworks: C#, ASP.NET MVC, .NET 8, Entity Framework Core, Angular, React, Java, Python, C,
HTML, CSS, JavaScript, SQL, VHDL, Verilog
Developer Tools & Technologies: Visual Studio, Visual Studio Code, SQL Server Management Studio, GitLab, GitHub,
Eclipse, JCreator, Postman, PgAdmin, Cisco Packet Tracer, Quartus, Unit Testing, JWT Authentication, Clean
Architecture, CQRS Pattern
Extracurricular
IEEE Club - German University in Cairo
Oct 2023 – Oct 2024
Head
Cairo, Egypt
• Fund Raising Team.

• Designing and integrating key components including speech-to-text (STT), natural language understanding and
generation, text-to-speech (TTS), and virtual environment interaction.
• Exploring publicly available datasets and constructing custom datasets to support dialectal and context-aware training
and evaluation.
• Targeting use cases in education and tutoring by enabling realistic, responsive, and dialect-aware spoken interaction
within immersive VR settings.

techniques, ZPF

Performed hands-on configuration of Layer 2/3 devices, enhancing practical troubleshooting efficiency

Implemented security policies to protect against common network threats, improving overall network
hardening

Cairo, Egypt

Dec. 2022 – March 2023
Projects
Network Security Analytics | Python
Random Signals and Noise, University Project
∗Data Engineering: Cleansed and standardized 30k+ records of network traffic data; utilized Pandas for
complex data transformations and NumPy for mathematical optimization.
∗Statistical Analysis: Applied SciPy to model Probability Density Functions and identify anomalies via Z-score
thresholding and multi-class fingerprinting.
∗Machine Learning: Built predictive models using Scikit-learn; conducted performance benchmarking using
Accuracy, Precision, and Recall metrics.
∗High-Performance Computing: Leveraged Joblib for parallelized execution of statistical distribution fitting.
Smart City IoT System - Automated People Counter | Arduino, Python
NETW503, University Project
∗Developed a real-time bi-directional people counter using an ESP32-based LilyGO T3 and dual ultrasonic sensors
with custom debounce logic for accurate direction detection.
∗Implemented a captive portal (AP mode) for persistent runtime configuration of Wi-Fi credentials, project
identity, and gateway IPs.
∗Built a multi-threaded Python Flask gateway acting as a centralized edge node, normalizing heterogeneous
sensor data and handling dynamic device provisioning via REST APIs.
∗Implemented secure JWT/Access Token management to unify telemetry streams into a real-time, scalable
ThingsBoard dashboard.
Automated Secure Remote MySQL Deployment | Bash
Giza Systems Internship
∗Built idempotent Bash automation to provision and secure MySQL 8 on RHEL 9 server and Ubuntu client,
applying secure scripting practices (traps, validation checks, etc.).
∗Implemented database creation, role-based access control, and privilege enforcement.
∗Configured remote connectivity (bind-address, firewalld, SELinux) with secure credential management.
∗Developed Ubuntu client automation with environment-based .cnf profiles and shell aliases.
Technical Skills
Languages: Java, Python, C++, SQL, Bash, Arduino, Intel 8086 Assembly
Developer Tools: Git, VS Code, Eclipse, Spyder, Arduino IDE, Docker, emu8086, ThingsBoard Cloud
Libraries: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, SoundDevice, Scikit-learn, Joblib
Operating Systems: Windows, Red Hat Enterprise Linux, Ubuntu, Nobara
Other Tools: Oracle VirtualBox, Cisco Packet Tracer, Wireshark, PingPlotter
Miscellaneous: Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Photoshop, Blender, OBS Studio
